purpose: Turn repeated install, runtime-hook, residue, evidence, and install-message failures into one release-grade safety contract.

principles: global common capabilities are reusable across projects
project-level complete projections are preserved and are not replaced by global skills
governance applies only to explicitly enabled directories
existing user configuration always wins over generated defaults

globalCommonCapabilityLayer.purpose: Reusable host-level capability library for meta-theory entry, foundational skills, plugins, commands, and dependency skills.
mustNotDo: enable Meta_Kim governance for every project automatically
treat optional third-party skill suite selection as required for project projection

projectCompleteProjectionLayer.purpose: Per-enabled-directory runtime surface generated from canonical sources and runtime mirrors.
projectCompleteProjectionLayer.selectionInvariant: Project bootstrap and install/update project sync must deploy only effective activeTargets. Repo default Enter resolves to claude,codex; explicit --targets or saved .meta-kim/local.overrides.json activeTargets may override the effective activeTargets. Cursor and OpenClaw project files appear only when explicitly selected.
projectCompleteProjectionLayer.mergePolicy: managed-block/add-only/preserve-user-state; never blind overwrite existing user files

directoryAuthorizationLayer.purpose: Only user-approved directories receive governance projection and project hooks.
directoryAuthorizationLayer.firstTriggerRequirement: dry-run plus native choice surface before apply unless trusted-auto is explicit
directoryAuthorizationLayer.unenabledProjectPolicy: global entry may report capability and dry-run plan, but must not write project files before confirmation

platformSupportTiers.boundary: OpenClaw and Cursor are the explicit formal project-projection compatibility targets, not the whole platform compatibility universe.
platformSupportTiers.promotionInvariant: dependency_install_target and candidate_probe products must not be treated as project projections until runtime profile, projection layout, generated target paths, sync tests, install policy, and live or official probe evidence exist.

installOptions.label: recommended
installOptions.behavior: install global common capability layer and generate/update the current project projection

installOptions.label: project only
installOptions.behavior: generate/update only the current project projection; do not write user home

installOptions.label: global only
installOptions.behavior: install global common capabilities only; do not ask for or write project directories

installOptions.label: advanced global hooks/rules/settings
installOptions.behavior: write Claude Code global hooks/settings only after a second explicit confirmation

noSkillSemantics.meaning: Skip optional third-party skill suite installation only.
mustNotSkip: project projection when project scope is selected
global meta-theory core sync when global scope is selected
host safety/control preservation such as Codex native choice-surface controls

sourceChain.runtimeMirrorSource: generated runtime projection folders inside the installed package root
sourceChain.projectTarget: current working project directory selected by --project-dir or host cwd

firstTriggerFlow: run dry-run probe from the global skill or command
present Claude Code AskUserQuestion or Codex request_user_input before apply when the runtime exposes the native choice surface
fall back only for compatibility runtimes and never call fallback a primary native popup
run apply only after user confirmation or an explicit trusted-auto policy
scenarioAcceptance: empty project dry-run exposes sourceChain and writes nothing
empty project apply creates project projections and manifest
current project dry-run after apply reports ready, requires no confirmation, and lists no project writes
existing user AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md keeps user text and adds a managed block
existing hooks and MCP config keep user entries and append Meta_Kim-managed entries
.codex/config.toml remains global-owned and is never copied into project bootstrap
stale project manifest reports stale before update
readonly or permission-denied apply returns failed/blocked and must not claim bootstrap pass
conflicting managed block is replaced only inside Meta_Kim markers
forbiddenOutcomes: silent project writes before dry-run and user confirmation
AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md blind overwrite when the target already has user content
claiming compatibility fallback as Claude Code or Codex native choice proof

requiredLayers.description: The prompt-submission payload or dependency hook output before runtime adaptation.
Runtime-specific adapter transformation that preserves prompt text and separates policy from context.
The runtime hook event and config entry that actually invokes the adapter.
The exact field consumed by the model, separate from UI-visible notices.

rules: Raw user input and optimized prompt bodies are fenced before display.
Codex adapter emits additionalContext, not systemMessage.
Host registration must be global for reusable HookPrompt and project-local only for project governance hooks.

runtimeEvidence.completionRule: Do not call host-side runtime changes complete without local assertion, host-side or installed-user self-test evidence, and review pass or explicit blocked reason.

success.meaning: The requested action completed or an existing destination is already usable.

skipped.meaning: The action was intentionally not run because it is optional, already satisfied, or not selected.
skipped.userNextAction: continue unless the skipped optional capability is needed

manual.meaning: The host requires a manual UI or command step that Meta_Kim cannot safely automate.
manual.userNextAction: perform the named manual action, then rerun the check

failed.meaning: The requested action did not complete and needs intervention or retry.
failed.userNextAction: fix the reported cause before declaring install or update complete
